gate_type: capability check only (view_woocommerce_reports or cap_export_orders) — no nonce on no-tab dispatch path
reasoning: The only gate protecting ajax_export_download_bulk_file() is WordPress authentication plus the view_woocommerce_reports or cap_export_orders capability, held by shop_manager and administrator roles by default. No nonce, secret, or unguessable token is required. The gate value is the victim's own session cookie, which is automatically attached by the browser in any cross-origin request — the attacker needs no separate value. Confirmed by: (1) ajax_gate() performs capability check then routes to the method directly on the no-tab path (class-wc-order-export-admin.php:530-538) with no nonce check; (2) the nonce check at lines 546-548 is in the ELSE branch only, unreachable on this path; (3) ajax_export_download_bulk_file() contains zero nonce verification; (4) the legitimate bulk-action redirect at line 597 constructs the URL as admin-ajax.php?action=order_exporter&method=export_download_bulk_file&export_bulk_profile=now&ids=... with no nonce parameter, proving design intent to work without one; (5) phpcs:ignore WordPress.Security.NonceVerification.Recommended at line 68 of the ajax class confirms deliberate omission. An attacker lures a shop_manager or administrator to a malicious page; the victim's browser sends a request to that URL; the capability check passes via the session cookie; build_and_send_file() executes unconditionally; order data is exported. No secret value needs to be obtained or guessed. Severity remains MEDIUM (requires luring a privileged user, not freely exploitable by any unauthenticated visitor).
